title: Decimals in expanded form
description: Practice converting decimals between standard form and expanded form.
topic_path: Module 1: Place value and decimal fractions
Topic B: Decimal fractions and place value patterns

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(2\times10)+\left ( 2\times\dfrac{1}{10} \right )+\left (9\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )+\left ( 4\times\dfrac{1}{1{,}000} \right )$

**Represent the expression as a decimal number.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{20}+\tealE{0.2}+\purpleE{ 0.09} + \maroonE{0.004}$ into standard form:

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueE{2}$| $0$|$.$  | $\tealE{2}$ | $\purpleE{9}$ | $\maroonE{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(3\times10)+(4\times1)+\left ( 5\times\dfrac{1}{10} \right )+\left (5\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )+\left ( 6\times\dfrac{1}{1{,}000} \right )$

**Represent the expression as a decimal number.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{30}+\goldE{4}+\tealE{0.5}+\purpleE{ 0.05} + \maroonE{0.006}$ into standard form:

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueE{3}$| $\goldE{4}$ |$.$  | $\tealE{5}$ | $\purpleE{5}$ | $\maroonE{6}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(9\times100)+(8\times10)+\left (7\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )$

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{900}+\maroonE{80}+\tealE{0.07}$ into standard form:

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueE{9}$| $\maroonE8$| $0$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\tealE7$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(8\times100)+(2\times1)+\left(4\times\dfrac{1}{10}\right)+\left (3\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )$

**Represent the expression as a decimal number.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{800}+\tealE{2}+\purpleE{0.4} + \maroonE{0.03}$ into standard form:

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueE{8}$| $0$| $\tealE{2}$| $.$  | $\purpleE4$  | $\maroonE{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(4\times1{,}000)+(3\times100)+\left (6\times\dfrac{1}{10} \right )$

**Represent the expression as a decimal number.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{4{,}000}+\tealE{300}+\purpleE{ 0.6}$ into standard form:

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths 
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueE{4}$| $\tealE{3}$| $0$ | $0$ | $.$ | $\purpleE6$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(9\times1{,}000)+(7\times100)+(1\times10)+(3\times1)+\left ( 4\times\dfrac{1}{10} \right )$

**Represent the expression as a decimal number.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{9{,}000}+\tealE{700}+\purpleE{10}+\goldE{3} + \maroonE{0.4}$ into standard form:

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths 
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueE{9}$| $\tealE{7}$| $\purpleE1$ | $\goldE3$ | $.$  | $\maroonD4$

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the expression below.

$(1\times1{,}000)+(3\times100)+(5\times10)+\left ( 9\times\dfrac{1}{10} \right )+\left (8\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )$

**Represent the expression as a decimal number.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's write the products in parentheses as decimals first, then add.

Let's use a place value chart to put $\blueE{1{,}000}+\tealE{300}+\goldE{50}+\purpleE{ 0.9} + \maroonE{0.08}$ into standard form:

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\blueE{1}$|  $\tealE3$ | $\goldE5$ | $0$ | $.$  | $\purpleE9$ | $\maroonE{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $8$ ones and $72$ thousandths?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $8$ ones and $72$ thousandths.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\goldD{8}$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleD{7}$ | $\maroonD{2}$

So, 
$\goldD8.0\purpleD7\maroonD2 =\goldD{8}+\purpleD{0.07}+\maroonD{ 0.002}$
$=\goldD{(8\times1)}+\purpleD{\left ( 7\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )}+\maroonD{\left (2\times\dfrac{1}{1000} \right )}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $1$ ten, $3$ ones, and $9$ thousandths$?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $1$ ten, $3$ ones, and $9$ thousandths.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\purpleC1$| $\blueD{3}$ |$.$  | $0$ | $0$ | $\greenD{9}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $2$ hundreds, $6$ tens, and $804$ thousandths?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show  $2$ hundreds, $6$ tens, and $804$ thousandths.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\redD2$| $\purpleC6$| $0$ |$.$  | $\greenD8$ | $0$ | $\goldD{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $809$ thousand and $45$ hundredths?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $809$ thousand and $45$ hundredths.

Hundred Thousands | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-: | :-:
$\purpleC8$ | $0$| $\blueD9$ |$0$ |$0$ | $0$ |$.$ | $\greenD4$ | $\goldD{5}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $6.087$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show $\goldD6.0\purpleD8\maroonD7$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
| $\goldD{6}$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\purpleD{8}$ | $\maroonD{7}$
So, 
$\goldD6.0\purpleD8\maroonD7 =\goldD{6}+\purpleD{0.08}+\maroonD{ 0.007}$
$=\goldD{(6\times1)}+\purpleD{\left ( 8\times\dfrac{1}{100} \right )}+\maroonD{\left (7\times\dfrac{1}{1000} \right )}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $57.142$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show 
$\redD5\purpleC7.\blueD1\greenD4\goldD2$.

Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\redD5$| $\purpleC{7}$ |$.$  | $\blueD1$ | $\greenD{4}$ | $\goldD{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is the same as $400.902$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to show 
$\purpleC400.\blueD90\greenD2$.

Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths| Thousandths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\purpleC4$ | $0$| $0$ |$.$  | $\blueD9$ | $0$ | $\greenD{2}$

Let's use a place value chart to show 
$\purpleC3\blueD6{,}000.0\greenD6$.

Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\purpleC3$ | $\blueD6$| $0$ | $0$| $0$ |$.$  | $0$ | $\greenD{6}$
